rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PATRICIA VALENTINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name and case number. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The Settlement Agreement says $280,929.54 is to be distributed to Patricia's Estate on or before 9/28/2020. The I&A lists assets as of Decedent's date of death 06/16/2019. Schwab bank statements appear to show the estate received the funds 9/30/2020. 2) Leland Valentine was appointed Administrator after a hearing with $300,000.00 bond. The I&A's allege assets of $1,132,939.92. See LRSF 14.16 A. Duty to Apply for Order Increasing Bond and LRSF 14.11 B. Examiner requested legal authority for not applying for an increase in bond. In response petition alleges an application to increase bond cannot be made because the parties must comply with the 02/18/2021 Court Order approving the Settlement. 3) It appeared Administrator has kept assets over $250k limit in a Schwab account. In response Petitioner alleges the Schwab account is a sweep account that distributes the funds into Schwab-Affiliated Banks that are FDIC insured up to $250,000.00. The Bank Statement provided dated 09/01/2020 - 09/30/2020 shows $594,764.85 in Charles Schwab Bank and $249,000.00 in Schwab Premier Bank. 4) Petitioner requests a $10,000.04 reserve.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SEAN SHARMA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Item 6d of the petition for probate is marked (indicating that Decedent was survived by issue of grandparents), but Item 8 lists siblings. 2) No declination to serve has been filed by Decedent's siblings. Petitioners allege that they attempted to contact them on several occasions but have been unsuccessful to date. 3) Decedent's parents have not been identified. Declarations filed on 3/22/21 identify a Keshav Dev Sharma and Krishna Sharma and describe due diligence efforts to locate them, but do not specify their relationship to Decedent. 4) Petitioners request no bond and indicate that assets are unknown. Attachment 2 of the petition indicates that they have located $200 in a bank account and stock issued by a corporation that Decedent owned, the value of which is uncertain. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 9th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTOPHER CARDENAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/14/2021. 1) The petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice to the Franchise Tax Board was mailed pursuant to Probate Code 9202(c). [LRSF 14.36 I. 2)] 2) Notwithstanding the waiver of account, if the petition and report requests statutory commissions or fees based on any amount other than the amount of the Inventory and Appraisal, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705 (b)] 3) No waiver of account has been filed. 4) No request for dismissal of the 5/17/2021 "duplicate" petition has been filed. Please email examiner at bslykhous@sftc.org to request the duplicate petition be taken off calendar. 5) The caption of the proposed order must begin with Order for Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6) b.]. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/28/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MATTIE B. LYONS REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For: Determination That The Mattie B. Lyons Revocable Trust Is Invalid And Imposition Of Constructive Trust-Undue Influence.
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/20/2021 1) There is no such filing as a Supplement to Clear Probate Notes. An amended pleading or amendment is required. (See CRC 7.3). The Supplement to Clear Probate Notes makes changes to errors in the petition. Changes to errors in the petition must be filed as an amended petition or an amendment. (CRC 7.3). Prior Examiner issues 1-3 do not need to be addressed in the amendment or amended petition. The caption of the proposed order should match what is request in the petition. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. (CRC 7.53). An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/6/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOUISA THEADORA MARIA VAN VELZEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/20/2021. 1) Petitioner must file proof of publication. 2) Petitioner must file Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121) 3) Petition must file a Duties and liabilities of personal Representative that includes the phone number. See page 2 #2. 4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] 5) Please explain and provide legal authority for writing tbd - trust assets of parents on page 2 under #3.d. (1). 6) Petitioner is not included in the petition on page 4 under #8 please explain and indicate the name and relationship to decedent, age, and address. 7) All heirs must be listed even if a decedent died testate. If the decedent had no known heirs, a declaration to that effect must be filed, setting forth the basis for that conclusion and the efforts made to locate heirs. [LRSF 14.18 C. 1)]. 8) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor(s) through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named, the relationship(s) to the decedent stated, and the date(s) of death specified. [LRSF 14.18 C. 3)]. 9) The date of death must be specified for all deceased devisees and heirs. [LRSF 14.18 D. 1)] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/6/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANDREW ALVIN BURLEIGH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/14/2021. 1) Notice must be re-done. Petitions for probate of lost wills must clearly state on their face that the will is lost and both the published and mailed notice must so state. [LRSF 14.20]. 2) Petitioner must file a Duties and Liabilities that includes petitioner's phone number. See page 2 #2. 3) Attachment 3f(3) does not address all the elements of Probate Code 6124. Evidence will be required to overcome the presumption of revocation. [LRSF 14.20]. 4) The date of death must be specified for all deceased devisees and heirs. [LRSF 14.18 D. 1)]. 5) Examiner cannot view filed Confidential Statement as of 6/11/2021. This will be reviewed prior to the next hearing. 6) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/28/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
